ci(ghcr-push): use workflow-scoped GITHUB_TOKEN, drop GHCR PAT + visibility-patch (#1254)

* ci(ghcr-push): authenticate crane push with workflow-scoped GITHUB_TOKEN

Replace the SOPS-decrypted long-lived GHCR PAT with the short-lived
`secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` GitHub Actions provisions per workflow run, and
delete the post-push visibility-patch workaround that the PAT required.

Mechanism:
  * push-images.yml now forwards GHCR_TOKEN=${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    and GHCR_USERNAME=x-access-token via bb-remote's env_overrides.
  * bb remote propagates those as
    `x-buildbuddy-platform.env-overrides=GHCR_TOKEN=...,GHCR_USERNAME=...`
    platform properties. BuildBuddy's ci_runner / executor
    (enterprise/server/remote_execution/executorplatform/executorplatform.go
    :319 in buildbuddy-io/buildbuddy) applies them as real env vars on
    the runner VM's command. The py_binary invoked by `bazel run` inherits
    them, so ghcr_push_lib.py's crane.login sees the workflow token.
  * Because the caller is secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN, new packages created by the
    push are automatically linked to this repo and inherit its (public)
    visibility. That obviates `_ensure_package_public`'s post-push
    PATCH /user/packages/container/<name> call, which existed only because
    the PAT didn't trigger auto-inheritance. Deleted the function, the
    GhcrImage.ghcr_token field, and the associated urllib/json imports.

Secret surface cleanup:

  * devinfra/secrets/ci_env.sh stops exporting GITHUB_TOKEN, GHCR_USERNAME,
    and GHCR_TOKEN. The agentydragon-agent machine PAT in
    secrets/github-pat-agentydragon-agent.yaml is still used by non-CI
    consumers (web_env.sh, devinfra/claude/hook_daemon, wt/server), so the
    PAT file itself stays. TODO comments left in ci_env.sh document this
    and mark secrets/ci/ghcr-credentials.sops.yaml as unused after the
    migration (can be deleted once no out-of-tree consumer depends on it).
  * setup-ci-secrets/action.yml drops GITHUB_TOKEN, GHCR_USERNAME, and
    GHCR_TOKEN from both the masking loop and the GITHUB_ENV export grep.

Verified:

  * bb remote env-override propagation path traced end-to-end in the
    BuildBuddy OSS source (see commit rationale + buildbuddy-io/buildbuddy
    enterprise/server/util/ci_runner_util/ci_runner_util.go +
    enterprise/server/remote_execution/executorplatform/executorplatform.go).
  * `bbr build //devinfra/ghcr_push/... //airlock:push_ghcr
    //grocy/auth_proxy:push_ghcr` succeeds on RBE (lint aspects included).

What still has to happen on first push: the PR has to merge to `devel`
before the new flow runs in anger, because workflow-token auth only kicks
in inside a real push-images.yml run. No backfill needed for existing
public packages — only NEW packages benefit from auto-visibility, and
`airlock`, `grocy-auth-proxy`, etc. are already public.

BAZEL_TEST_INVOCATIONS=none: workflow + shell + Python plumbing change; no test target covers the modified paths (verified by `bbr build //devinfra/ghcr_push/... //airlock:push_ghcr //grocy/auth_proxy:push_ghcr`).

commit.message:
Fix HTML.KpiCard: replace invalid DOUBLE param type with STRING VAL pattern

Root cause: TMDL does not support DOUBLE as a function parameter type.
The function silently failed to load, causing DAX to report 'The syntax
for HTML is incorrect' on every calling measure.

Changes:
- Remove yoyChange:DOUBLE param from HTML.KpiCard (invalid TMDL type)
- Change all params to STRING VAL (matches PowerofBI.IBCS.Helpers.Title)
- Derive positive/negative status via LEFT(yoyPercentFmt,1)<>"-"
  so no numeric parameter is needed
- Remove _yoyChange arg from all 3 HTML Card calling measures
- Restore definition.pbir to byConnection (was overwritten to byPath)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: harden auto-sync scheduler against crash and stop-race issues

Address code review findings from Opus and GPT-5.2:
- Wrap entire executeAutoSync() in outer try/finally so scheduleNext()
  is always called even on pre-try errors (prevents scheduler death)
- Add .catch() on setTimeout callback to prevent unhandled promise
  rejection from crashing the Node.js process
- Add 'stopped' flag so stopAutoSync() prevents reschedule when a
  sync run is in-flight
- Only release sync lock if it was actually acquired

commit.message:
Implement spec-compliant ResolveExport for module resolution validation

Add validate_module_resolution() with the full ResolveExport algorithm
(ES2024 §16.2.1.16.3) to detect module link-time errors:
- Circular re-exports (resolve set cycle detection)
- Missing named re-export bindings (returns null)
- Ambiguous star-export bindings (multiple different resolutions)

Key spec nuances handled:
- import-then-export reclassification: 'import {x} from m; export {x}'
  is traced through to the import source as an indirect export entry
- Namespace re-exports: 'export * as ns from m' and
  'import * as ns from m; export {ns}' both resolve to the target
  module's namespace binding, enabling correct ambiguity comparison
- Star exports skip 'default' per spec step 9

Uses ModuleResolutionContext struct to thread per-module maps:
local_exports, import_bindings, export_to_local, all_reexport_deps.

commit.message:
Phase 2: Handshake Protocol Types and Transport Trait Extension (#142)

* feat: add handshake protocol types and Transport trait extension

Phase 2 of the version-compatibility implementation plan.

Adds handshake types for version negotiation between cluster nodes:

system_actors.rs:
- HandshakeRequest: node_id, wire_version (WireVersion), app_version, adapter
- HandshakeResponse: Accepted { ... } | Rejected { reason, detail }
- RejectionReason: IncompatibleProtocol, IncompatibleAdapter
- validate_handshake(): compares local vs remote config, returns response
- 8 unit tests (accept, reject protocol/adapter, priority order, field check)

proto/system.proto:
- HandshakeRequestProto, HandshakeResponseProto (oneof accepted/rejected)
- HandshakeAcceptedProto, HandshakeRejectedProto
- RejectionReasonProto enum
- All field numbers frozen per wire protocol convention

proto.rs:
- encode/decode_handshake_request: roundtrip with WireVersion parsing
- encode/decode_handshake_response: oneof Accepted/Rejected with validation
- rejection_reason_to_proto/from_proto conversions
- 8 proto roundtrip + validation tests

transport.rs:
- Transport::handshake() default returns error (safe for adapters not yet updated)
- InMemoryTransport: handshake_info map, set_handshake_info(), link() shares info
- InMemoryTransport::handshake() validates via validate_handshake()
- 5 transport handshake tests (accept, reject, no info, default trait)

lib.rs:
- Re-exports HandshakeRequest, HandshakeResponse, RejectionReason, validate_handshake

Total: 21 new tests, zero clippy warnings, all 506 existing tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address multi-model review findings for Phase 2

- Fix validate_handshake arg order in InMemoryTransport::handshake()
  (ChatGPT+Haiku+Gemini: response contained caller's info, not remote's)
- Add empty-field validation for wire_version/adapter in decode_handshake_response
  (Sonnet+Haiku: inconsistent with decode_handshake_request validation)
- Fix doc on set_handshake_info: info is copied at link time, not shared
  (ChatGPT+Gemini: post-link updates not visible to already-linked peers)
- Add 4 new proto validation tests (empty adapter, empty wire_version)
- Strengthen transport test: assert response node_id is remote, not caller

Private fields finding (ChatGPT) declined: Rust enum variant fields
are always public. No code change needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
modernize: Swift 6, @MainActor, Logger, clean platform types

GameLogic.swift:
- Add @MainActor isolation — safe to call directly from @MainActor UI code
- Replace OSLog/os_log with modern Logger API (subsystem + category)
- Remove LogLevel helper enum — Logger has native .debug/.info/.error methods
- Mark cachedWinningPatterns nonisolated(unsafe): written once per key in init,
  never mutated after; safe without a lock
- Add Sendable conformance to Player, GameState, MoveOutcome
- Simplify checkWin for-in with where clause
- Simplify getWinningPatternCoordinates using filter + map + sorted

GameScene.swift:
- Replace os_log + OSLog with Logger
- Remove NSCoder.string(for:) log arguments — use Swift string interpolation
- Remove GameEvent/GameTouchEvent type aliases (confusing indirection);
  use UITouch/UIEvent/NSEvent directly in #if os() blocks
- Use UIColor.systemRed/systemBlue/label/systemBackground/separator
  instead of hardcoded .red/.blue/.black/.white/.lightGray — respects
  dark mode and dynamic type automatically
- Add safe Array subscript extension to avoid unchecked index access
- Simplify resetGame() with array iteration over node names

TicTacToeTests.swift:
- Fix GameLogic.Player -> Player, GameLogic.GameState -> GameState
  (types are top-level, not nested inside GameLogic)
- Add @MainActor to test suite to match GameLogic actor isolation
- Mark static test argument arrays nonisolated(unsafe) to satisfy
  Swift 6 strict concurrency rules for @Test macro access

project.pbxproj:
- SWIFT_VERSION = 5.0 -> 6.0

All 16 tests pass. iOS build clean.
